title: Digital Video Broadcasting — Cable (DVB-C)
short description: DVB‑C is a cable‑based digital TV transmission standard that delivers MPEG transport streams over coaxial networks using QAM modulation. It was first published as ETSI EN 300 429 and defines the framing, channel coding, and modulation needed for reliable multi‑program distribution via cable. The system uses 16‑QAM up to 256‑QAM, allowing high spectral efficiency while maintaining very low error rates thanks to forward error correction. It has become the dominant global standard for digital cable TV outside North America.
description:
**DVB‑C** is a cable‑based digital TV transmission standard that delivers MPEG transport streams over coaxial networks using QAM modulation. It was first published as ETSI EN 300 429 and defines the framing, channel coding, and modulation needed for reliable multi‑program distribution via cable. The system uses 16‑QAM up to 256‑QAM, allowing high spectral efficiency while maintaining very low error rates thanks to forward error correction. It has become the dominant global standard for digital cable TV outside North America.

Transmission medium: coaxial cable networks designed for broadcast distribution
Modulation: QAM (16/32/64/128/256), enabling high data throughput
Coding: MPEG‑2 transport stream with FEC to achieve quasi‑error‑free performance
Use cases: multi‑channel digital TV distribution, including hundreds of millions of households worldwide

DVB‑C is often compared with DVB‑S and DVB‑T as part of the broader DVB family, which provides open standards for satellite, cable, and terrestrial digital broadcasting.
